method: direct economic utility ridge baseline from past-only analyzer summary + candidate action

train_fit.note: In-sample utility correlation is weak, and action selection mostly chooses SKIP on val.

val_sweeps.standard_thresholds: threshold >= 0 produced zero trades; threshold -0.0025 produced 7 losing trades.
val_sweeps.wide_thresholds: thresholds from -0.02 to -0.0025 still produced the same 7 losing trades for normal alphas.

diagnosis: A simple linear value scorer cannot extract a profitable action ranking from the current past-summary features. It is too conservative and mostly selects SKIP; when forced to trade, selected trades lose.
decision: Do not promote direct ridge value scoring. Next step is a pairwise/ranking learnability baseline to test whether best-vs-candidate ordering is learnable at all before training another LLM head.
